title: Processing Records for FOIA #2025-18307

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Processing Records for FOIA #2025-18307
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request all records related to the processing of FOIA Request Number 2025-18307. This request includes, but is not limited to:
​The complete administrative file for the specified request.
​All correspondence (internal and external, including emails and memos) regarding this request.
​Search documentation, including search memos, logs, and specific instructions provided to IRS staff to locate responsive records.
​Fee and referral records, including any fee assessments, fee waiver determinations, or documentation regarding referrals to other agency components.
​The final response letter and any list of withholdings or exemptions (such as a Vaughn Index) applied to the original request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

GabrielMorrow

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Shrewsbury Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Shrewsbury Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Shrewsbury Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Massachusetts Public Records Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on March 30, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Mr. Dankoff,

I have sent this email response to both the email you provided in the
request as well as the Muck rock email.  We have not had much luck
responding to Muck Rock emails which is why I sent this request to both
emails.


On 3/30/26, the department received you public records request for the
following:

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and
procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records
request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding:

* Officer or employee use of cellphones
* Officer use of social media
* Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages
* Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management
* Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices
* Bringing your own device

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have
been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025.

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing
mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information
about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic
Connect, etc.) that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone
and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department
mobile technology is centrally managed.

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is
responded to, between a department phone (including both department
provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers:

* (802) 872-6020
* (781) 359-7500
* (860) 244-0522
* (413) 221-1303
* (857) 416-3742
* (781) 359-7451
* (703) 725-5982
* (781) 359-7553
* (781) 917-5143
* (857) 416-3705
* (603) 493-4520

Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol
(CBP).


4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS,
RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued
mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded
to, that contain any of the following terms:

* ICE
* CBP
* Immigration
* Immigrant
* Detainer

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup
system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any
third-party vendor on behalf of the department.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in
the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible
format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for
persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen
readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical
documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you
would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I
would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if
available.


*Here is the Department's response to your request:*

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and
procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records
request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding:

* Officer or employee use of cellphones
* Officer use of social media
* Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages
* Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management
* Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices
* Bringing your own device

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have
been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025.

*Attached are several policies that cover employee use of cell phones,
officer use of social media and software and internet usage on department
issued devices.  Our retention practices of Department owned phone logs are
consistent with Massachusetts Records Retention policies which is 1 year.
 *

*The current cell phone policy was issued 12/8/25; The current Information
technology use policy was issued 11/21/25; The current Speech, Expression
and Social Networking Policy was issued 12/08/25.  **The other policy
attached is the 025 Computer Use Policy is the older policy that covered
software and internet usage on department issued devices prior to
11/21/25.  There was no Social networking or cell phone policy in place
prior to the current policy.     *


2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing
mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information
about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic
Connect, etc.) that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone
and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department
mobile technology is centrally managed.

*The Department has no records responsive for this request.  *

*All departmental IT systems are managed by a separate municipal entity
(SELCO IT). The Town of Shrewsbury purchases hardware and software via the
Commonwealth of Massachusetts IT47 and IT48 contracts. All other major
purchases will follow the open bid process as per Commonwealth procurement
laws.  *


3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is
responded to, between a department phone (including both department
provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers:

* (802) 872-6020
* (781) 359-7500
* (860) 244-0522
* (413) 221-1303
* (857) 416-3742
* (781) 359-7451
* (703) 725-5982
* (781) 359-7553
* (781) 917-5143
* (857) 416-3705
* (603) 493-4520

Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol
(CBP).

 *All call logs are managed by a seperate municipal entity (SELCO IT).  I
have attached two call logs (Verizon (Text and Cell) and Zoom (Landline))
from SELCO IT.    *


4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS,
RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued
mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded
to, that contain any of the following terms:

* ICE
* CBP
* Immigration
* Immigrant
* Detainer

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup
system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any
third-party vendor on behalf of the department.



*The Department has no records responsive to this request. *

Please be advised that pursuant to 950 CMR 32.00 and M.G.L. c. 66, s.
10A(a) you have the right to appeal this decision to the Supervisor
of Public Records within 90 calendar days.  Such appeal must be in writing
and should include a copy of your original request and if available, this
response.  Please note that the Supervisor may only consider appeals
arising from written public records requests.  Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66, s.
10A (c), you also have the right to seek judicial review by commencing a
civil action in Superior Court.


Should you have any questions please reach out to the Police clerk's
office.


Respectfully,

Sgt. Napolitano
--
Sergeant Larry Napolitano
Administrative Sergeant
Town of Shrewsbury
106 Maple Avenue
Shrewsbury, MA 01545

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Genesee County)

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Genesee County)
communications.communication:
Dear Genesee County Board of County Commissioners FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Genesee County Board of County Commissioners county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: [Invoice Created] Genesee County, Michigan public records request #26-538
communications.communication:
Genesee County, Michigan ************************************************************************ An invoice has been created for record request #26-538. To: Alexander Munro - MuckRock News FOIA Request #26-538 Please accept this correspondence in response to the public records request submitted to Genesee County. The records request was deemed officially recognized by me, Genesee County’s FOIA Specialist, on April 6, 2026, and it has been processed pursuant to the provisions of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MCL 15.231 et seq. I understand the records request to be for a copy of “county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format for Genesee County specifically requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price.” Please know that in responding to the records request, I worked closed with and relied upon information received from the Deputy Treasurer of the Genesee County Treasurers Office. The records request is granted. The fee for providing copies of records of a County Treasurer is determined by the Transcripts and Abstracts of Records Act, MCL 48.101 (the “TARA”), MCL 15.234(4); Title Office, Inc. v. Van Buren County Treasurer, 469 Mich. 516; 676 N.W.2d 207 (2004). The TARA sets the fee as follows: “for an abstract of taxes on any description of land, or an abstract with statement of name and residence of taxpayers, $0.25, but with a minimum of $0.50 per abstract. See MCL 48.101 Section 1 (1)(a) and (b); and MCL 48.101(3).” As of March 9, 2026, there are 16,973 records responsive to the records request. At the statutory cost of $0.50 per abstract, the fee for providing these records is $8,486.50. If you wish to receive these records, please make the check/money order payable to the Genesee County Treasurer, with FOIA Request #26-538 written on the subject line, and U.S. mail it to the below address: Genesee County Board of County Commissioners Attn: Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist 324 S. Saginaw Street Flint, MI 48502 Upon receipt of the payment, I will inform the Genesee County Treasurers Office to finalize the processing of request. I estimate a processing time of 10-business days from receipt of payment to release the records to you through the portal. As set forth under Section 4(14) of the FOIA, MCL 15.234(14), if a fee appeal has not been filed under Section 10 of the FOIA, the Genesee County FOIA Coordinator must receive the required fee within 45 days after the statutorily-determined receipt of this notice, which is May 22, 2026, otherwise the request will be considered abandoned and Genesee County will not be required to fulfill the request. Copies of Genesee County’s FOIA Policy and Procedures, along with its FOIA Summary, are online: https://www.geneseecountymi.gov/government/freedom_of_information.php. PORTAL INSTRUCTIONS (1) Payments cannot be accepted through the portal. Please kindly submit payment as instructed above. Respectfully. Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Genesee County, Michigan.

Genesee County, Michigan ************************************************************************ Record request #26-538 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: To: Alexander Munro - MuckRock News Request #26-538 This correspondence is an amended response for the public records request submitted to Genesee County’s FOIA Specialist. The request was deemed originally recognized on April 6, 2026, and it has been processed pursuant to the provisions of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MCL 15.231 et seq. *Genesee County offices were closed for business on Friday, April 3, 2026, for Good Friday. I understand the request to be for copies of "Genesee County Board of County Commissioners county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request. Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data." Please know that in responding to the request, I worked with and relied upon information received from Genesee County’s Equalization Director, Ms. Hayduk. The request is denied at this time. To the best of the Ms. Hayduk’s knowledge, information, and belief, under the information provided by you or by any other description reasonably known to Genesee County, the public records do not exist. A thorough search was conducted, and no responsive records were located per information received from the Ms. Hayduk, I hereby certify. In the spirit of transparency, please be advised that you may submit a new request seeking 2026 assessment data but not until after June 1, 2026, when the final State Equalization has been approved. Please know that there will be a cost associated with fulfilling the request To the extent this response constitutes a denial of the request, you have the right to submit a written appeal by sending an email to foiaappeals@geneseecountymi.gov, addressed to the chairperson of the Genesee County Board of County Commissioners, specifically stating the word “appeal” and identifying the reason or reasons for reversal of the disclosure denial. You are further advised that you have the right to seek judicial review of such denial and obtain attorney’s fees and damages to the extent provided in, and subject to the conditions of, section 10 of the FOIA (“Section 10”). A copy of Section 10 is uploaded to the portal and is incorporated in this response by reference. Copies of Genesee County’s FOIA Policy and Procedures, along with its FOIA Summary, are available online at: https://www.geneseecountymi.gov/government/freedom_of_information.php.   PORTAL INSTRUCTIONS To locate the "Section 10" document released by Genesee County through the portal, please click on the "Documents" tab located directly below the "Request" box. This is where you can view, download, and print the "Section 10" document released to you.   There was no charge for responding to this public records request. Respectfully. Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: Florida Sunshine Law Request: ECPA Valuation Justification For The Escambia County Equestrian Center

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Sunshine Law Request: ECPA Valuation Justification For The Escambia County Equestrian Center
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Dear Records Custodian,

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the administrative and appraisal records used to calculate the 2025 "Land Value" for Parcel ID 171S312101000000 (Situs: 7750 MOBILE HWY 32526).

Specifically, the certified roll data indicates the Land Value for this 151.4041-acre parcel was reduced from $1,513,800 in 2024 to $52,983 in 2025. I am requesting the specific documents that justify and authorize this $1,460,817 reduction.

Please provide:

The 2024 and 2025 internal appraisal worksheets or CAMA (Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal) system notes for this specific parcel.

Any notices of zoning changes, use-code reclassifications, or agricultural/conservation exemptions applied to this land between January 1, 2024, and the present.

Any interagency memorandums, environmental assessments, hazard notifications, or federal/state easement filings received by the Property Appraiser’s office regarding this parcel that negatively impacted the land valuation model.

If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

The 2024 and 2025 internal appraisal worksheets or CAMA (Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal) system notes for this specific parcel.

Any notices of zoning changes, use-code reclassifications, or agricultural/conservation exemptions applied to this land between January 1, 2024, and the present.

Any interagency memorandums, environmental assessments, hazard notifications, or federal/state easement filings received by the Property Appraiser’s office regarding this parcel that negatively impacted the land valuation model.

If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

title: Jackson County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Jackson County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kierstyn Aubrecht
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 20, 2026

100 Court St. North                                                                                                                 
Ripley, WV 25271

To Whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Jackson County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely,
Kierstyn Aubrecht
kra00019@mix.wvu.edu

Cc: Christina Gossett <Christina.Gossett@jacksoncountywv.com>
Subject: FOIA Jackson County, WV Federal Opioid

 

CAUTION: This email originated from outside our organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. Please contact the IT Department with questions.

 

Dear Ms. Aubrecht,

 

I represent the Jackson County Commission.  I am in receipt of your recent FOIA request regarding the federal opioid suit.  Jackson County opted out of the federal opioid suit and was only involved in state litigation.  Therefore, we did receive any opioid funds from the federal opioid settlement. 

 

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me.

 

Eric Holmes, Esq.

Law Offices of Harris & Holmes, PLLC

115 North Church Street

Ripley, WV 25271

Fax: 304-372-7042

If the reader of this message is not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any dissemination, distribution, or copy of this communication is strictly prohibited.  If you have received this communication in error, please contact the sender by reply Email and destroy all copies of the original message.  Thank you.

 

WARNING! WIRE FRAUD ADVISORY: Wire fraud and email hacking/phishing attacks are on the rise! If you have a transaction with us and you receive an email containing wire transfer instructions, please do not reply to the email. We will never send wire instructions through email."

title: February 5, 2019 Child Recovery Incident

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: February 5, 2019 Child Recovery Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Related to a reported incident involving the recovery of minor children by law enforcement in El Paso, Texas on or about February 5, 2019.

Specifically, I request:

• Incident reports
• Offense reports
• Arrest records (if any)
• Dispatch / CAD logs
• Any reports or logs referencing coordination with the U.S. Marshals Service
• Any records relating to custodial interference, missing children, welfare checks, or recovery of minors

This request pertains to records involving one or more of the following individuals:

• Daniel Gary Chandler (also known as Daniel Gary Salmon)
• April Chandler
• Bertha Salmon

including any records where these individuals are listed as subject, reporting party, victim, or associated person.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Wayne Whitney

title: ALPR Records

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: ALPR Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires, is anticipated.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

LPL

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia School District/SAU30)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia School District/SAU30)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The final report of the Under Secretary of Defense For Intelligence and Security following its review into Elon Musk's security clearance and his compliance with federal reporting protocols, as recently discussed in the New York Times:  https://www.nytimes.com/2024/12/17/technology/elon-musk-spacex-national-security-reporting.html

Please include any other reports investigating Elon Musk's security clearance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Silverman

title: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of all reports, lists, databases, or individual documents created since 1/1/16 detail lawsuits brought against the city's police department. This should include any documentation mechanism your department uses to track lawsuits. It should also include any documents your department submits to local, state, or federal agencies listing the number and status of lawsuits brought against the police department. This request should include documents compiled quarterly, annually, or in other increments and documents or databases that contain responsive information over a longer period. 

- The records should include, if it exists, the following information: 
Settlement amounts
Case category (civil rights, collisions, etc) 
Case type/description (harassment, crowd control, in-custody death, etc)
Involved officers
Case numbers
Total expenses (this should include the legal costs to the city, including outsite litigation costs.) 

- Any corrective action reports that describe the incident that caused the lawsuit and the corrective actions taken to minimize risk, prevent recurrence, and hold the department and the involved officers accountable. The reports are often sent to respective bodies to approve settlements (city council, board of supervisors, controller, or risk management office) or created by another office within the city or county. 
- Copies of settlement agreements 

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv). California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.

or created by another office within the city or county.
- Copies of settlement agreements

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights
violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv).
California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist
in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform
me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer
the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I
look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.

RE: FOIL Records Request of October 26, 2025, Reference # R001200-102625 
Dear Noam Perry, 
CUNY University Central Office received a FOIL request from you on October 26, 2025. You requested: Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

- Email communications from January 1, 2020 and the current date, between your organization and Palantir Technologies Inc. 
- Lists of company presenters for career fairs or student recruitment events between January 1, 2020 and the current date. 

We give the above time frame because the company tends to engage in multi-year contracts. 

The College has determined that portions of your request can be denied based on the following: 
We are denying your request for email communications as your request fails to reasonably describe the records sought. Pursuant to Section 89(3)(a) of the Public Officers Law, requested records must be "reasonably described" in order to prevent an agency from going to unreasonable lengths in order to comply with the Freedom of Information Law request. Based on the language of your request, it is unclear whose e-mail communications you're specifically seeking from "your organization" and which e-mail communications you're specifically seeking. Further, your request fails to include essential information such as specific custodian(s) or an e-mail domain from Palantir Technologies Inc., which is an external organization from CUNY University Central Office, the search terms for the e-mail communications you seek, or any information regarding the subject matter or types of e-mail communications you are seeking. As such, CUNY University Central Office is unable to conduct a search for the e-mail communications you seek. 
Regarding your request for lists of company presenters for career fairs, CUNY University Central Office has reviewed its files and has located the responsive record to your request. Please log in to the FOIL Records Access Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive records. FOIL Records Request - R001200-102625 
Accordingly, your request for records is granted in part and denied in part, and the requested records are available in the records center for your review. Certain portions have been redacted, and/or certain records have not been provided to you based on the explanation above. 
If you disagree with any ruling made in connection with this request, you can appeal to the CUNY’S FOIL Appeals Officer by filing your appeal through the CUNY FOIL portal or by following the instructions listed on CUNY’s FOIL website. 
Sincerely, 
Records Access Officer 
CUNY University Central Office

title: Sunshine Law Request - Missouri State Generative AI Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Sunshine Law Request - Missouri State Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to Missouri state government use of generative AI tools overseen by the Office of Administration, including internal chatbot prototypes and government-secured AI models, as described in Executive Order 26-02 (Jan 2026).

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI or other models hosted in Missouri's secured tenant, internal chatbot prototypes, and any other generative AI chatbot tools used by Missouri state agencies for the period from April 7, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

title: CPRA Request: Research Funding, DSP Operations, and Employment Records (UC Berkeley, 2022–2026) (Office Of The President (ucop), University Of California)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Research Funding, DSP Operations, and Employment Records (UC Berkeley, 2022–2026) (Office Of The President (ucop), University Of California)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I request records reflecting research funding received by Professor Sheri L. Johnson at UC Berkeley from 2023 to 2026, including:

Total amounts and funding sources
Grant agreements or award documents
Budget summaries and expenditure categories (e.g., personnel, equipment, travel, overhead)
Any reports or documents reflecting allocation or use of such funds

I am not requesting personally identifiable information; aggregated or redacted records are sufficient.

I also request aggregated records (2023–2026) regarding the Disabled Students’ Program (DSP), including:

Total funding received (including government sources) and annual budgets
The number of accommodation requests, approvals, denials, and fulfillment rates
The number of complaints alleging failure to provide accommodations, including outcome categories
Aggregated, de-identified demographic breakdowns (e.g., age ranges, nationality categories), where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information; aggregated or redacted data will suffice.

I further request records related to DSP operations during the 2022–2023 labor action involving DSP advisors, including:

Communications, reports, or policy documents regarding service disruptions
Aggregated or de-identified complaint summaries related to DSP accommodation failures during this period

Additionally, I request employment-related records for Marilyn de la Cruz, including:

Employment contracts or offer letters
Job descriptions and role responsibilities
Salary ranges or compensation structure, where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information or confidential personnel evaluations; redacted or aggregated records will suffice.

Lastly,
I request records of communications between UCOP and UC Berkeley regarding:

The processing and handling of my CPRA requests
Administrative coordination related to my Government Claim and demand letter

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Franklin School District/SAU18)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records related to the California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement between GO-Biz and Atomic Machines, Inc. (https://www.atomicmachines.com). This agreement was widely reported and can be found online here: https://business.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/Atomic-Machines-Inc.-CCTC-Agreement.pdf

Agreement dated approximately November 2025):

Compliance Reports: All annual compliance reports, worksheets, and supporting documentation submitted by Atomic Machines, Inc. for Taxable Year 2025 (and 2026 to date) to demonstrate the achievement of milestones related to net increase of full-time employees and investment amounts.

Milestone Evaluations: Any written determinations or correspondence from GO-Biz verifying that Atomic Machines, Inc. has met its "Project/Milestone" requirements as set forth in Section 3 and Exhibit A of the Agreement.

Recapture or Breach Notices: Any records, notices, or internal memoranda discussing potential "Events of Breach" (pursuant to Section 10 of the Agreement) or the "Recapture" of tax credits due to the company’s failure to maintain hiring or investment targets.

Wage Verifications: Redacted payroll summaries or certifications provided by the company to verify it has met the "Minimum Annual Wage" and "Cumulative Average Annual Wage" commitments for California full-time employees.

Relocation Disclosures: Any communications from the company regarding its certification that, absent this credit, it would have "terminated all or a portion of its employees in California or relocate[d]... to another state.

This request is made in the public interest to ensure transparency regarding the allocation of $15,000,000 in taxpayer-funded credits - especially considering the reputation of Atomic Machine's leadership.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communication, to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School District, are governmental records available to the public upon request.  Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.  However, confidential information about students, staff, and parents may not be subject to disclosure.  The confidentiality of information about students and personnel matters must be maintained by the individual or entity to which this e-mail is addressed.  Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or distribution of confidential information is prohibited.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in San Bernardino County in that year. Alameda County has an example of these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.communication:
San Bernardino County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-30627: Dear Aleksandra, A Cast Vote Record Report that contains ballot data only and is void of any voter information may be extracted and compiled. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Reports (not ballot images) the County will need to extract and compile the data. It is estimated it will take ROV staff approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2022 election and approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2024 election. The fee for this process is $310.13 per hour. The total amount estimated to fulfill your request is $620.26. The records for the report you have requested for the November 2022 election are currently set for destruction in accordance with Elections Code section 17300 et seq. in approximately 10 days. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election please submit the fee of $310.13 within the next 10 days to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters at 777 E. Rialto Ave., San Bernardino, CA 92415. Once the Registrar of Voters has received your payment for either the November 2022 election, November 2024 election or both, the County will begin to extract and compile the information requested. If payment is not received within the allotted 10 days, the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election will be destroyed as scheduled. Sincerely, Thalia Amador Elections Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at San Bernardino County.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-30640
communications.communication:
Hi there,

Thank you for the timely and helpful information, and for giving me advanced notice regarding the timeline for the destruction of the November 2022 election records. I unfortunately was not able to get back to you in a timely manner, and so I understand the records are now permanently out of reach. Thank you for alerting me in advance that this may happen.

I'm writing now to followup about the November 2024 election records. I would like to proceed with my request for those records, assuming they are still available, and am happy to pay the associated fee you outlined in your original response. 

When you have a chance, could you please confirm how I can submit payment to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters for the fee of $310.13? Is it only possible to pay through mailed check? Or is there an alternative way to process the payment online? Please let me know what is best. 

Thank you again for your patience, and your most diligent assistance with my request(s).  

All my best,
Aleksandra

title: Request for Court Records and Business Licsenses for Griffin Brown (City Of Reno)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Request for Court Records and Business Licsenses for Griffin Brown (City Of Reno)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026.
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Madison Kitch

communications.subject: General Public Records :: G020071-040726
communications.communication:
Dear Madison : 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Reno. 
Your request was received in this office on April 07, 2026 and given the reference number: #G020071-040726 for tracking purposes.  Request Details: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Madison Kitch 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259946/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate City department(s) to locate the information requested. Pursuant to NRS 239.0107 we will notify you within five (5) business days of receipt of the anticipated date your records will be available, and any estimated cost to produce them. Please note any confidential, proprietary, or protected information will be redacted prior to the release of your records. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Again, thank you for using the City of Reno Public Records Center.

Madison Kitch

Sincerely, 
City of Reno 
City Clerk's Office Please note any confidential, proprietary, or protected information will be redacted prior to the release of the records. There could be a charge for copies of public records. If the estimated cost of the copies requested is $25.00 or more, the requester will be required to pay in full prior to reproduction. Materials will be held for 14 days. If not retrieved, the requester will be charged in full for a second reproduction in addition to any unpaid original charges. Advance payment will be forfeited if material is not retrieved.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

Axon - Winthrop Harbor Police Department   
    
   
     
 Axon - Winthrop Harbor Police Department  
    
 
  
Evidence.com - Evidence Download Link 
 
  
 Ricardo Luevanos (Badge ID: 320) from Winthrop Harbor Police Department (https://winthropharborpdil.evidence.com) has sent you a link to download evidence on Evidence.com. After clicking on the link below you will download 1 zip or iso files containing the requested evidence. This download may take a while depending on your internet connection. 

Good Afternoon,

Attached is part 1 of our response to your request for records dated September 1, 2025. 

Thank you,

Records Clerk, R. Luevanos # 320 
Please note that your access to this link will expire on April 16, 2026 13:48:02 (-05:00). 
Afterwards, you will need to contact the sender to request access.

The Axon Team 
 
 SECURITY NOTICE: Axon will not send you emails that request your username, password, or any other sensitive information. title: Bend Flock Records 2024-2026

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Bend Flock Records 2024-2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am looking for a copy of all public records previously produced from 2024 to the present and related to the use of Flock and any other Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs) in the City, including cameras used by the Bend Police. Previously produced records may include maps, contracts, user agreements, audit logs, and other similar items. By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you have any questions. This request is per ORS 192.001 to 192.990.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

communications.subject: City of Bend Police Department - Police Department Request PD-2026-1183: Completed
communications.communication:
RE: Request Number PD-2026-1183 submitted on 04/05/2026 
  
 Dear Southern Oregon Flock Off, 
  
 Attached please find the responsive records for your public records request. The City of Bend’s FLOCK cameras were installed beginning in early 2025 and fully discontinued as of January 8, 2026. The records attached were previously provided to another requestor and include the following:  
  
 1.    Flock Agreement 
  
 •    October 10, 2024, signed Agreement between the City of Bend and Flock Safety, an amendment dated July 21, 2025. 
  
 
2.    Training Materials 
  
 •    The City of Bend Police Department provided in-person training on the Flock program. The attached Flock Camera Basic Training document was referenced during the training sessions. 
  
 •    Additional training was completed via the Flock website: 
  
 https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdEiOhV2sGCNd-2BqfzgTS7Y22w4RRDYgGbRd4GeF8CiI5VuCI_XQpzFalsfwP6vLLSanJsMEc2kpb-2F6Xz-2FaOVhzPohSMHtUdwF4WA6xsGs00JBz9L4e4QN9pXhWU9s4n-2FQsHDb-2BokPSyFzJiGxmTJ7T1FP1Cj6DEzw3XMrO1a-2BRIyRSnHk2HXQD0f9GirabPwk8zHtrW9GU56A1QgylAxUWR8kN6v68iV5DJnKcc3DhmMcj3G1zZYuIblIr0AQml9ioJLNh4GeauFii3sy4l0YmUWOznskvwlCA5M-2FM0DdqQhQx8KYhdymRpBlOthvSvSdrTpOSi-2B19DDB7DIZMCku54Ij6PcuEMUloTdJWC0-2BRn6A2VGe 
  
 
3.    Organization Audit of ALPR Searches (June 2025 - December 2025) 
  
 •    Included columns: Org Name, Total Networks Searched, Total Devices Searched, Time Frame, Reason, Case#, Filters, Search Time, and Search Type. 
  
 •    Information in the "Reason", "Case#", and "Filters" columns have been redacted in accordance with applicable ORS public records exemptions. 
  
 
4.    Network Audit of ALPR Searches (June 2025 - December 2025) 
  
 •    Included columns: Org Name, Total Networks Searched, Total Devices Searched, Time Frame, Reason, Case#, Filters, Search Time, and Search Type. 
  
 •    Information in the "Reason", "Case#", and "Filters" columns have been redacted in accordance with applicable ORS public records exemptions. 
  
 
5.    Records of Organizations with Shared ALPR Cameras 
  
 
6.    City of Bend's Flock Administrative Settings 
  
 •    Administrative settings within the FLOCK program for the Bend Police Department as of December 3, 2025. 
  
 
7.    Locations of where the ALPRs were located in Bend as of 12/2/2025. 
  
 

The request Request Number: PD-2026-1183 has been completed. Please click on the request link to download the response documentation. 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 


City of Bend Police Department 
  
 555 NE 15th St 
  
 Bend, OR 97701

title: LIHTC & MTBA Documents

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

(1) A log or table of the LIHTC 9% competitive submissions/applications for 2018 (equivalent to the posting that exists for other years like 2020 at https://s3.amazonaws.com/thda.org/Documents/Business-Partners/Multi-Family-Developers/LIHTC-Program/2020-Competitive-Submissions-for-Website.pdf).
(2) The same kind log or table for the 2019 submissions. 
(3) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2024 (equivalent to the posting, for round 1 of 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/MTBA-Round-1-Applications.pdf).
(4) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 1 of 2023.
(5) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2023.
(6) A log or table of the MTBA Closings in 2025 (equivalent to the posting, for the year 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2024-MTBA-Listing-for-Website-Closing_Carryforward-1.pdf).

Note that the first five of these documents pertain to applications submitted, not awards allocated. For context, also note that this request was preceded by an attempt to locate these documents on the THDA website. Files were successfully located for other years, but not for those requested above. For #4, a document was found, but the version on the website does not include a THDA # (project ID), which is unusual. The documents sought in this request are expected to include this field, if it at all exists.

In case any of the six logs of applications/closings only appear as part of a larger spreadsheet or database extract that covers several years or rounds, such a record would also be considered valid toward satisfying this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Lars Brown.

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
Mr. Hoover,

Our office received your email, regarding your request for records associated with a recent LIHTC project.  We can accommodate your request if it is in compliance with the Tennessee Public Records Act, found in Tennessee Code Annotated, Title 10, Chapter 7.  The policy states that the Requestor must be “a citizen of the State of Tennessee”. Our Public Records policy can be found at https://thda.org/about-thda/publicrecords

I have attached THDA’s Public Records Request Form which is needed to initiate the process.  We will need you to complete the Request Form, sign and date it.  We will also need proof in the form of a Photo ID issued by a governmental entity including the Requestor’s Tennessee address.  Please note, your request must be specific in nature. If requesting paper copies, the costs for any records and/or labor costs if applicable, will be disclosed to you after we determine the number of documents requested.

We will be happy to answer any questions you may have about this process.


Respectfully,

Jenia Tortora
Paralegal

Tennessee Housing Development Agency
Andrew Jackson Building
502 Deaderick Street, 3rd Floor
Nashville, Tennessee 37243
P. 615-815-2027

Charity Miles Williams | Assistant Chief Legal Counsel

title: Total value of outstanding bus balances (Dallas Area Rapid Transit)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Total value of outstanding bus balances (Dallas Area Rapid Transit)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

What is the total accounts payable of mass transit passes? That is to say what is the total outstanding value of all bus passes?
